sections.heading: Overview
sections.paragraph: In a significant advancement for targeted protein degradation technologies, Rayca Precision and Data Powered Therapeutics have announced a strategic co-development partnership aimed at designing and advancing a new generation of nanobody therapeutics for cancers of high unmet need. This collaboration integrates Rayca’s computational discovery engine, which models therapeutic targets in motion and designs candidate molecules using structure-aware generative methods, with Data Powered Therapeutics’ platform for high-throughput experimental validation. The partnership specifically targets difficult oncology settings, including resistant cancers and aggressive neuroendocrine cancers such as small cell lung cancer, utilizing a nanobody bioPROTAC approach alongside other mechanisms. By addressing developability liabilities like expression and stability early in the design phase, the companies aim to resolve attrition risks before clinical progression. While specific targets remain confidential, the agreement focuses on advancing lead programmes toward development candidates with first-in-class potential. This initiative underscores the industry shift toward combining AI-driven design with automated wet-lab characterization to accelerate the delivery of differentiated therapeutics for resistant tumor types, ensuring candidate quality is moved upstream to resolve liabilities before they become critical issues during development.

points.text: Rayca Precision — Nanobody bioPROTAC — Cancers of high unmet need — Strategic co-development partnership
Rayca Precision: Strategic objective to treat developability as a primary at the design stage using modelling and collective intelligence for nanobody therapeutics.
Data Powered Therapeutics: Strategic objective to automate wet-lab data generation at scale to compress sequential work into automated runs for nanobody variants.

Mechanism: Targeted protein degradation through a nanobody bioPROTAC approach. Population: Patients with cancers resistant to standard therapy, tumors evading immune system, aggressive neuroendocrine cancers such as small cell lung cancer. Status: Lead programmes advancing toward development candidates. Specific targets remain confidential.
Rayca Precision and Data Powered Therapeutics entered a strategic co-development partnership to design and advance nanobody therapeutics for cancers of high unmet need.
